question:
Deebo Samuel Sr.: Anytime Touchdown

Description: In the upcoming NFL game, scheduled for November 30 at 8:20 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Deebo Samuel Sr. scores a touchdown at any point during regulation or overtime.

This market will resolve to "No" if Deebo Samuel Sr. does not score a touchdown during regulation or overtime.

Qualifying Touchdowns:

- A rushing touchdown by the player

- A receiving touchdown caught by the player

- A special teams touchdown (e.g. punt/kick return)

Passing touchdowns do not qualify. Defensive touchdowns will be credited to the defensive unit, not individually credited to the player who scores.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take a snap, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NFL game book as published on NFL.com.

Resolution source: https://www.nfl.com/scores

This market will resolve to "No" if Deebo Samuel Sr. does not score a touchdown during regulation or overtime.

evidences.content: Analysis: Samuel was deemed a non-participant Wednesday and Thursday, so his presence at Friday's practice is a positive sign ahead of Sunday's game against the Cowboys, especially after he played through the injury last week to the tune of 72 percent of the offensive snaps, though it remains to be seen how much work he'll get in. In any case, the Commanders' upcoming injury report will reveal the wideout's official participation level as well as his Week 7 game status. ##### Sits out practice again Samuel (heel) did not practice Thursday. [...] Analysis: San Francisco will release its second Week 4 injury report later Thursday, when the extent of Samuel's participation will be known. Samuel is back on the field for the first time since suffering a right calf strain in the 49ers' Week 2 loss to the Vikings, and while the expectation remains that he'll miss a second straight contest this Sunday versus the Patriots, his presence at practice suggests he can't be ruled out at this stage. ##### Not practicing Wednesday San Francisco head coach Kyle Shanahan said Samuel (calf) won't practice Wednesday, Eric Branch of the San Francisco Chronicle reports. evidences.title: Fantasy Football Start/Sit for Week 13
evidences.content: ### Deebo Samuel, WR, Washington Commanders 2025 has been a forgettable season for the Commanders. Injuries haven’t helped, but this just feels like a lost year. Unfortunately, things won’t get better in Week 13 as they face the Broncos, who sport a top-five defense and allow the fewest fantasy points to wide receivers. Deebo might be one of the few serviceable options on this team, but he still belongs on your bench this week. ### Colston Loveland, TE, Chicago Bears [...] ### Daniel Jones, QB, Indianapolis Colts Daniel Jones started 2025 hot, as did the Colts as a team, but things have cooled off fast. Jones has put the ball on the ground six times and thrown four interceptions over his last two games. This isn’t a good spot to rebound. The Giants face the Houston Texans, who are a top-two defense and allow the fewest fantasy points to quarterbacks. Leave Jones on the bench and stream elsewhere. ### Brock Purdy, QB, San Francisco 49ers [...] I always like to include a deeper dart throw, and here it is. The Lions defense has been getting absolutely torched through the air in the second half of the season and currently allows the eighth-most fantasy points to wide receivers. Some...

evidences.title: Injury roundup: 49ers WR Deebo Samuel (rib/oblique) expected to play vs. Buccaneers
evidences.content: Samuel was limited in all three practices this week due to the injuries, leading to the questionable designation on Friday. While these rib and oblique ailments were new, Samuel has dealt with his fair share of injuries so far this season. First it was a calf injury for Week 3 and 4, then a wrist injury that coincided with a bout of pneumonia-like symptoms that landed Samuel in the hospital ahead of Week 8, though he returned for that game. Despite the various maladies, Samuel has only missed one game -- Week 3 versus the Rams -- and is not expected to miss another in Week 10. Through seven games played, Samuel has 24 receptions for 406 yards and a touchdown, averaging 16.9 yards per catch. [...] NBA superstar LeBron James is officially a free agent, with the Philadelphia 76ers one of his rumored landing spots. Eagles RB Saquon Barkley pitched basketball's best on joining Philly's NBA franchise. ### Titans to curtail Jeffery Simmons' snaps to keep star DT fresh for key moments The Tennessee Titans plan to reduce Jeffery Simmons' snaps to ensure he's at full force for big moments. Simmons has played at least 80% of available snaps since 2020. ### Zach Allen expects Broncos to bre...

evidences.title: WR Deebo Samuel Sr. Back at 49ers HQ as 49ers Open Up Cowboys Week
evidences.content: San Francisco 49ers logo # WR Deebo Samuel Sr. Back at 49ers HQ as 49ers Open Up Cowboys Week Author Image Team Reporter The San Francisco 49ers Wednesday injury report had nine names of players either sidelined from practice or limited due to injury. Left tackle Trent Williams was No. 10 on the list but was only sitting out of practice on account of a veteran rest day. With wide receiver Brandon Aiyuk (ACL, MCL) being officially placed on the Injured Reserve list earlier in the day, and both Deebo Samuel Sr. (wrist/illness) and Jauan Jennings (hip) listed as non-participants in the team's first practice of the week, San Francisco is currently without its top three wide receivers headed into "Sunday Night Football" versus the Dallas Cowboys. [...] Wide receiver Chris Conley, the remaining veteran on the team's 53-man roster, is also working through an injury of his own. He suffered an ankle sprain against Kansas City and was limited in Wednesday's practice. The team will need to lean on younger and rookie wideouts in their final game before the Bye week to spark production on the offense. That group includes first-round pick Ricky Pearsall, fourth-rounder Jacob Cowing and sophom...

evidences.title: Week 13 Fantasy Football Start 'Em, Sit 'Em | Fantasy Points
evidences.content: #### FLEX Plays Deebo Samuel – After a midseason heel/foot injury, Deebo looked closer to full health in the Commanders last two games before their bye week (11/103/2 receiving on 13 targets). Deebo has earned at least 20% of the Commanders' targets in six out of his last 8 games. Now that McLaurin and Noah Brown are both back from injury, it will allow Deebo to play in the slot full-time. #### Sit ‘Em Terry McLaurin – Welcome back! After another long layoff due to a quad injury, McLaurin returns to face Patrick Surtain. Brutal. Marcus Mariota – We’ve liked Mariota as a streaming option over the midseason but this is clearly a spot to avoid against this Broncos defense that is allowing a league-low by passing FP/DB (0.28). Jayden Daniels (elbow) will try to return in Week 14. [...] Tyler Warren – While this is a tough matchup for the Colts WRs, I expect that Warren will be highly involved. Houston has been mixing in more man coverage as of late, but they’re still predominantly a zone-based defense (77%). This bodes well for Warren. He leads the team in targets (0.26) and yards (2.63) per route run vs. zone coverage. Warren has been far less efficient when facing man coverage (0....
